competitors.label: Scaling Up
competitors.selectionReason: Vue scaling-up guidance is the canonical baseline for tooling and structural decisions in larger Vue apps.
theirStrengths: Scaling Up is a direct, credible comparator for this topic.
theirGaps: Scaling Up is less compressed around the specific interview framing and likely follow-up confusion this page targets.

realWorldUseCases:
Overview

“Scalability” in Vue isn’t just about performance — it’s mainly about keeping complexity manageable as features and teams grow. Early architectural choices determine whether your codebase stays modular, testable, and predictable.
actionableExamples:
Recommended baseline for a scalable Vue 3 app

• Vite + Vue 3 + TypeScript
• Vue Router with lazy-loaded routes
• Pinia for true shared state only; keep UI state local
• Composition API + composables for shared logic
• Feature-based folder structure (domain modules)
• Central API layer (services) + consistent error handling
• Vitest for unit/component tests + Playwright/Cypress for E2E
• ESLint/Prettier + typecheck in CI
followUpConfusion:
Overview

“Scalability” in Vue isn’t just about performance — it’s mainly about keeping complexity manageable as features and teams grow. Early architectural choices determine whether your codebase stays modular, testable, and predictable.

realWorldUseCases: Scaling Up covers the same topic directly, but our shipped answer is more explicit about the production or interview decision boundary.
actionableExamples: Scaling Up includes a credible baseline explanation or example, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Scaling Up is less direct on the likely follow-up confusion than our shipped answer.

actionableExamples: Add one tighter worked example later if this page needs more separation on the example axis.

competitors.winReason: Scaling Up is relevant coverage, but our page is stronger because it turns the concept into an interview-ready decision model while keeping the example coverage competitive.
